extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won Stage 10 with his most dominant sprint, coming from far back with Sabatini's leadout after Quick-Step received no chase cooperation.
tactical_patterns: Teams refused to help Quick-Step chase, allowing Kittel perfect setup—non-cooperation becomes viable counter-tactic against dominant sprint trains.
Sabatini released Kittel before final corner rather than through it, allowing acceleration space to prevent wheel-surfing before top speed.
Sky rode defensive front positions on flat sprint stage despite no GC threat, treating transition stages as injury-risk management days.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Winning by unprecedented margins (fourth of ten stages) suggests peak form timing incomparable to past Tours
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: negative: Could not hold Kittel's wheel despite perfect positioning, revealing pure power gap not tactical deficit
rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: negative: Lotto worked entire stage for Greipel who finished 12th, indicating sprint form collapse not leadout failure
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: 50th career day in yellow reveals comfort level allowing Sky to prioritize crash avoidance over positioning aggression
